lead: The ongoing discourse around climate change is increasingly intersecting with politics and technology, highlighting the complexities of environmental impact and economic implications.

stories.title: Have politics finally come for the National Academies of Science?
stories.summary: A new report on climate attribution from the National Academies of Science is stirring concerns about political influence over scientific findings. This development raises questions about the integrity of climate science and the potential for political agendas to shape critical environmental research. The implications of this report could affect public policy and funding for climate initiatives.

stories.title: Data centre boom could send power prices 26 per cent higher: Climate group
stories.summary: As Australia invests heavily in data centres, a climate group warns that this boom could lead to a 26% increase in power prices for households. The report highlights the environmental costs associated with expanding digital infrastructure, suggesting that a shift towards a more energy-intensive grid could exacerbate climate issues. This situation underscores the need for sustainable practices in technology development.
